title: St. Antony Church, Ujire
tagline: St. Antony Church, Ujire — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: St. Antony Church, Ujire is a much-loved spiritual town in Karnataka, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Antony Church is a Catholic church in Ujire, within the Belthangady Varado of the Diocese of Mangalore, India. As of October 2021, the parish had 261 families and 1097 members. The current church was built in 1969.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ujire
topPlaces.description: Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.

topPlaces.name: Kalmanja village
topPlaces.description: Kalmanja is a village in the Belthangady taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. Kalmanja has around 2000 people, most of whom are farmers and agriculturists.

topPlaces.name: Jamalabad
topPlaces.description: Jamalabad Fort is an old hilltop fortification in Killoor road, Belthangady, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kudremukh range of hills, 8 km north of Beltangady town and 65 km from the city of Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Dharmasthala Temple
topPlaces.description: Dharmasthala Temple (Shri Kṣhētra Dharmasthala) is an 800-year-old Hindu religious institution in the temple town of Dharmasthala in Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. The deities of the temple are Hindu god Shiva, who is referred to as Mañjunatha, Hindu goddess Ammanavaru (meaning mother), the Tirthankara Chandraprabha and the protective gods of Jainism, Kalarahu, Kalarkayi, Kumarasvami and Kanyakumari.

topPlaces.name: Eparchy of Belthangady
topPlaces.description: The Eparchy of Belthangady is an Eastern Catholic eparchy in Belthangady, India, under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church. It was established in 1999 by Pope John Paul II's bull "Cum Ampla"And appointed Mar Lawrence Mukkuzhi as its first bishop[1999-2025].

topPlaces.name: Tulu Nadu
topPlaces.description: Tulu Nadu, or Tulunad, is a region and a proposed state on the southwestern coast of India. The region is predominantly inhabited by the Tulu people, also known as Tuluva, who speak Tulu, a Dravidian language.

topPlaces.name: South Canara
topPlaces.description: South Canara (South Kannada) was a district of the Madras Presidency of British Raj, located at 13.00°N 75.40°E / 13.00; 75.40. It comprised the towns of Kasaragod and Udupi and adjacent villages, with the administration at Mangalore city.

topPlaces.name: Dharmasthala
topPlaces.description: Dharmasthala () (earlier known as Kuduma) is an Indian temple town on the banks of the Nethravathi River in the taluk of Belthangady of the Dakshina Kannada or southern district in Karnataka, India. The town is known for its centuries-old Dharmasthala Temple, devoted to the Hindu god Manjunatha.

items.note: Ujire is a town near Dharmasthala in Karnataka, India. It is in Belthangady taluk of Dakshina Kannada district.
Kalmanja is a village in the Belthangady taluk in Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. Kalmanja has around 2000 people, most of whom are farmers and agriculturists.

Jamalabad Fort is an old hilltop fortification in Killoor road, Belthangady, Dakshina Kannada District, Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kudremukh range of hills, 8 km north of Beltangady town and 65 km from the city of Mangalore.

Dharmasthala Temple (Shri Kṣhētra Dharmasthala) is an 800-year-old Hindu religious institution in the temple town of Dharmasthala in Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka, India. The deities of the temple are Hindu god Shiva, who is referred to as Mañjunatha, Hindu goddess Ammanavaru (meaning mother), the Tirthankara Chandraprabha and the protective gods of Jainism, Kalarahu, Kalarkayi, Kumarasvami and Kanyakumari.

The Eparchy of Belthangady is an Eastern Catholic eparchy in Belthangady, India, under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church. It was established in 1999 by Pope John Paul II's bull "Cum Ampla"And appointed Mar Lawrence Mukkuzhi as its first bishop[1999-2025].

Tulu Nadu, or Tulunad, is a region and a proposed state on the southwestern coast of India. The region is predominantly inhabited by the Tulu people, also known as Tuluva, who speak Tulu, a Dravidian language.

South Canara (South Kannada) was a district of the Madras Presidency of British Raj, located at 13.00°N 75.40°E / 13.00; 75.40. It comprised the towns of Kasaragod and Udupi and adjacent villages, with the administration at Mangalore city.

Dharmasthala () (earlier known as Kuduma) is an Indian temple town on the banks of the Nethravathi River in the taluk of Belthangady of the Dakshina Kannada or southern district in Karnataka, India. The town is known for its centuries-old Dharmasthala Temple, devoted to the Hindu god Manjunatha.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Antony Church, Ujire?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Antony Church, Ujire is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Antony Church, Ujire?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Antony Church, Ujire?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Antony Church, Ujire at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Antony Church, Ujire?
faq.a: Stays in St. Antony Church, Ujire start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Antony Church, Ujire?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ujire, Kalmanja village, Jamalabad, Dharmasthala Temple, Eparchy of Belthangady.
